title: Princeton Changes Its 133-Year-Old Honor Code Over AI Cheating Fears
snippet: "I was a straight A student" is about to come off bumper stickers. The sooner we realize that AI also might stand for ASSESSMENT INSURRECTION (sorry, maybe you can do better), the faster we will get on the road to invigorating the classroom and the degree.
match_reason: social (social_post) — Post is authored by Ann Kirschner (linkedin.com/in/annkirschner), adding original commentary on AI-driven changes to academic integrity policy at Princeton.

title: 5 Tips for Assignment Design in the Agentic AI Age
snippet: UCLA computational biology lecturer Tina Austin argues that as agentic AI tools can complete assignments end-to-end, faculty must stop grading outputs and start grading thinking — with concrete redesign strategies for assessment in the AI era.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — Directly addresses AI's impact on teaching, learning, and academic integrity assessment at universities, authored by a UC faculty member.

title: Writing Is Proxy v. Writing Is Thinking: What Both Get Wrong About Generative AI
snippet: USC writing professor Patricia Taylor argues that generative AI uniquely offloads language generation itself — not just mechanics — risking cognitive deskilling, and calls for careful policy on AI use in writing-intensive courses.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — Addresses the pedagogical and academic integrity implications of generative AI in university teaching and learning.

title: 'More Everything Forever' and Universities as Counterweight to the Dominant AI Narrative
snippet: Joshua Kim argues that universities must develop their own institutional AI strategies and values rather than uncritically adopting Silicon Valley's dominant AI worldview, as entanglement with AI companies deepens across higher education.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — Addresses AI governance and institutional strategy for universities, relevant to the committee's charge on responsible AI policy.

title: Artificial Intelligence Policy – UC Berkeley Law (Effective Summer 2026)
snippet: UC Berkeley School of Law's new AI policy, effective Summer 2026, bans AI use in exams and credited coursework, citing the need to develop lawyers' own cognitive skills while acknowledging future lawyers may need AI fluency.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — UC campus-level AI academic integrity and assessment policy directly within the committee's mandate on AI in teaching and learning.

title: Owens Announces Hearing on Higher Education in the Age of AI — 'Building an AI-Ready America'
snippet: House Subcommittee on Higher Education and Workforce Development announced and held a June 3, 2026 hearing titled 'Building an AI-Ready America: Higher Education in the Age of AI,' the seventh in a series examining AI's impact on higher education.
match_reason: websearch (press_release) — Federal legislative hearing on AI in higher education signals national policy landscape directly relevant to UC AI governance and strategy.
